summary: Iteration 3 (LLM-driven CEO via Claude Sonnet 4.5 + multi-agent collaboration) fully verified end-to-end. Backend: 15/15 pytest pass — new fields (mode, rationale, agent_runs[], used_llm) present on /api/orchestrate, backwards-compat fields (chosen_agent_id/name, team_id/name, output) correctly mirror agent_runs[0], explicit agent_id forces mode='single' + used_llm=false, multi-purpose directive 'Launch a new product…' correctly produced sequential 3-agent plan via real LLM (verified used_llm=True, mode=sequential, agents=[user_researcher, ad_copywriter, social_publisher]). Frontend: header pill 'CEO · CLAUDE SONNET 4.5' visible; all 6 new slug testids (launch-a-product, ship-a-landing-page, close-a-deal, draft-a-prd, seo-audit, weekly-report) present and clickable; launch-a-product chip triggers full multi-agent flow with terminal logs 'Consulting Claude · drafting multi-agent plan…', 'Plan ready · mode=sequential · 3 agent(s) · <rationale>', '→ Handing off to <Agent> (<Team>): <instruction>', '<Agent> Acknowledged. Working on it…', '<Agent> Output ready. Delivering to CEO.', 'All agents reported back. Mission complete.' streaming in order; OutputDialog shows '3-Agent Plan' header, 'Sequential pipeline' badge, CEO rationale with 'claude · sonnet 4.5' tag, and 3 numbered output-run-{0,1,2} cards each with instruction + output; free-text directive 'plan a podcast launch including audience research and outreach' also routes through LLM and produces 3-agent plan with claude tag.

issues: Pill 'CEO · CLAUDE SONNET 4.5' uses 9px font on fuchsia/10 background — readable but borderline on bright displays. Consider bumping to 10px or raising opacity.
Team name shown as small uppercase subtitle under agent name — good. Could optionally tint it with the team color for stronger multi-team signal.

action_items: OPTIONAL: minor design polish on header pill contrast and OutputDialog team color tint (non-blocking).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is now 571 lines; still a single file. Recommend splitting in next iteration: data/teams.py (TEAMS, DUMMY_OUTPUTS), services/ceo.py (LLM plan + fallback + JSON parsing), models.py (Pydantic). Crosses the 500-line guideline.

_extract_json regex `\{.*\}` with DOTALL is greedy and could over-match if Claude returns prose containing braces — current Claude output is clean JSON so it works, but worth bounding via balanced-brace parser for robustness.
In orchestrate(), if a plan step's agent_id resolves to None it is silently `continue`'d — could leave agent_runs shorter than plan_steps without surfacing the issue. Consider logging a warning.
execute_task_dummy is intentionally mocked (per user choice) — clearly documented. No action.

ceo_plan_with_llm catches all exceptions and returns None to trigger fallback. Good resilience, but the fallback yields used_llm=false silently — UI does already differentiate via the 'claude · sonnet 4.5' tag, so this is fine.
MongoDB client is still instantiated at import time but unused. Defer or remove until persistence is added.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (all iteration-3 flows verified incl. real LLM call returning 3-agent sequential plan)

test_credentials: N/A — no auth in this app.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend pytest at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py covers: TestTeams (4 teams, 20 agents), TestOrchestrateResponseShape (new fields + agent_runs well-formed + backwards-compat mirror), TestOrchestrateLLMPlan (multi-agent directive, simple directive, steps log structure incl. handoffs/working/success), TestOrchestrateExplicitAgent (parametrize 5 agents force single+used_llm=false, 404, 400 empty/whitespace). LLM key was confirmed live this run — sequential 3-agent plans returned for product launch and podcast launch directives. Frontend testids relevant to iteration 3: 'CEO · CLAUDE SONNET 4.5' pill text, quick-task-{launch-a-product,ship-a-landing-page,close-a-deal,draft-a-prd,seo-audit,weekly-report} (slug-based), output-run-{0..N}, output-dialog, output-close, command-input, command-submit. Multi-agent UI flow takes ~10-15s end-to-end (LLM 3-8s + replay of ~10 step logs at 480ms each). MOCKED: execute_task_dummy still returns hardcoded outputs (intentional). Consulting-Claude log appears at ~T+1.2s after submit so a brief poll/wait is needed to detect it.
rca of the issue: No bugs found. Iteration-3 LLM-CEO + multi-agent collaboration is fully implemented and working with real Claude Sonnet 4.5 calls plus a working keyword-fallback path.
